---
title: 'What if that fight was ultimately unfruitful?'
date: '2020-02-16T18:26:00.0010000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: '(My Journal) Fed By Faith'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '8196657358998213915'
blogger_filename: '/2020/02/what-if-that-fight-was-ultimately.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/(My Journal) Fed By Faith/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Musings'
  - 'Politics'
---

<div>Politics are intriguing...&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>We all have wildly different experiences, perspectives, even understanding of actual history.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>One person sees an event from a wildly different view than another. One person suffered while another lived victorious during the same era. Each sees his or her own perspective and those they sympathize with...&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>Then you see worldview playing it's role. A person who believes in Objective God given Truth, irrelevant of experience, will make a different decision than one who believes in relativism and "everyone had their own truth".&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>Yet we all live in one giant melting pot where we must synthesize these views into one society. Therefore, nobody gets their way each and every time.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>What I am struck with just now, is that it doesn't, ultimately, matter. None of it.</div><div><br></div><div>The world is headed on a death spiral, and nothing we do will stop that.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>We can, however, love people. Even people who are flat wrong, or insane, or crazy.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>We can love well, regardless of how vehemently we disagree on even basic facts about reality.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>We all see in part, know in part, see as through a fogged over mirror.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>There is coming a day, where we will each see just how incomplete our worldview was, regardless of how tightly we held it, or thoroughly we researched it.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>Selah.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;">
  <a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/1581877602597312-0-f1bac12743a8.png" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;">
    <img border="0"   src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/1581877602597312-0-f1bac12743a8.png" width="400">
  </a>
</div><br></div>
